Name: Caterpie
Effects: Search your deck for a Caterpie and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

Name: Metapod
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can’t withstand a harsh attack.

Name: Butterfree
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.

Name: Shroomish
Flavor-Text: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

Name: Breloom
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Impact Blow.
Flavor-Text: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

Name: Breloom V
Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does that much more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pansage
Flavor-Text: It’s good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It’s kind enough to share them with friends.

Name: Simisage
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

Name: Sewaddle
Effects: Discard a [G] Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

Name: Swadloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself from the cold by wrapping up in leaves. It stays on the move, eating leaves in forests.

Name: Leavanny
Effects: Heal all damage from each of your Benched Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: It keeps its eggs warm with heat from fermenting leaves. It also uses leaves to make warm wrappings for Sewaddle.

Name: Maractus
Effects: Discard any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They’re jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert.

Name: Shelmet
Flavor-Text: It has a strange physiology that responds to electricity. When together with Karrablast, Shelmet evolves for some reason.

Name: Accelgor
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack can be used for [G].
Flavor-Text: Discarding its shell made it nimble. To keep itself from dehydrating, it wraps its body in bands of membrane.

Name: Virizion
Effects: Put up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Legends say this Pokémon confounded opponents with its swift movements.

Name: Phantump
Flavor-Text: With a voice like a human child’s, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.

Name: Trevenant
Flavor-Text: Small roots that extend from the tips of this Pokémon’s feet can tie into the trees of the forest and give Trevenant control over them.

Name: Grubbin
Effects: Attach a [L] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.

Name: Dewpider
Flavor-Text: Dewpider normally lives underwater. When it comes onto land in search of food, it takes water with it in the form of a bubble on its head.

Name: Araquanid
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It acts as a caretaker for Dewpider, putting them inside its bubble and letting them eat any leftover food.

Name: Tsareena V
Effects: You may discard any number of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rillaboom V
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rillaboom VMAX
Effects: This attack also does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Gossifleur
Flavor-Text: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

Name: Eldegoss
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

Name: Appletun V
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zarude
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Once the vines on Zarude’s body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

Name: Vulpix
Flavor-Text: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

Name: Vulpix
Flavor-Text: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

Name: Ninetales
Flavor-Text: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

Name: Ninetales
Ability-Effect: The Retreat Cost of each of your Pokémon that has any [R] Energy attached is [C][C] less.
Flavor-Text: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Extremely loyal, it will fearlessly bark at any opponent to protect its own Trainer from harm.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that has long been admired for its beauty. It runs agilely as if on wings.

Name: Slugma
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn’t keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

Name: Magcargo
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each tails, discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

Name: Victini
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

Name: Pansear
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Simisear
Effects: Discard up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

Name: Chandelure V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 40 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chandelure VMAX
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t play any Pokémon Tool cards from their hand.
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 70 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: Attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack also does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

Name: Oricorio
Ability-Effect: All of your Fusion Strike Pokémon take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can’t apply more than 1 Lesson in Zeal Ability at a time.
Effects: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: This Oricorio has drunk red nectar. If its Trainer gives the wrong order, this passionate Pokémon becomes fiercely angry.

Name: Cinderace V
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cinderace V
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cinderace VMAX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Sizzlipede
Flavor-Text: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they’re well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

Name: Sizzlipede
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they’re well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

Name: Centiskorch
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s attacks do 90 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

Name: Centiskorch
Flavor-Text: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

Name: Shellder
Flavor-Text: Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.

Name: Cloyster
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Once it slams its shell shut, it is impossible to open, even by those with superior strength.

Name: Staryu
Effects: Attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: No number of injuries can bother Staryu. Its amazing regenerative powers return it to its previous state in half a day!

Name: Starmie
Effects: Discard any amount of [W] Energy from this Pokémon. Then, for each Energy card you discarded in this way, choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon and do 30 damage to it. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) This damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It rotates its geometrically shaped body to swim through the water. It always seems to be sending out mysterious radio waves.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

Name: Totodile
Flavor-Text: Its powerful, well-developed jaws are capable of crushing anything. Even its Trainer must be careful.

Name: Croconaw
Flavor-Text: Once it bites down, it won’t let go until it loses its fangs. New fangs quickly grow into place.

Name: Feraligatr
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you must flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 5 cards of your opponent’s deck. If tails, discard the top 5 cards of your deck.
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

Name: Marill
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let’s All Rollout attack.
Flavor-Text: Even after Marill swims in a cold sea, its water- repellent fur dries almost as soon as Marill leaves the water. That’s why this Pokémon is never cold.

Name: Azumarill
Effects: Put up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon and Supporter cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it’s in the water.

Name: Qwilfish
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: The small spikes covering its body developed from scales. They inject a toxin that causes fainting.

Name: Mantine
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As it majestically swims, it doesn’t care if Remoraid attach to it to scavenge for its leftovers.

Name: Mudkip
Flavor-Text: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

Name: Marshtomp
Effects: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Living on muddy ground that provides poor footing has made its legs sturdy.

Name: Swampert
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [W] Energy card or a [F] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

Name: Clamperl
Flavor-Text: Clamperl’s pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder’s pearls.

Name: Huntail
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Single Strike Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] more.
Flavor-Text: Deep seas are their habitat. According to tradition, when Huntail wash up onshore, something unfortunate will happen.

Name: Gorebyss
Ability-Effect: Your opponent’s Rapid Strike Pokémon in play have no Abilities.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It sucks bodily fluids out of its prey. The leftover meat sinks to the seafloor, where it becomes food for other Pokémon.

Name: Panpour
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

Name: Simipour
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 60 damage for each Supporter card you find there.
Flavor-Text: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.

Name: Basculin
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known for their violence, these Pokémon have the most fights with schools of red-striped Basculin.

Name: Galarian Darumaka
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The colder they get, the more energetic they are. They freeze their breath to make snowballs, using them as ammo for playful snowball fights.

Name: Galarian Darmanitan
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack can be used for [W].
Flavor-Text: Though it has a gentle disposition, it’s also very strong. It will quickly freeze the snowball on its head before going for a headbutt.

Name: Greninja V
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Clauncher
Flavor-Text: It moves around by jetting water from its right pincer. It has a poor sense of balance, so it’s terrible at swimming straight.

Name: Clawitzer
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its right arm is packed with meat. When its pincer falls off, it’s exported to be used as a cooking ingredient.

Name: Crabominable V
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pyukumuku
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your hand, you may reveal it and put it on the bottom of your deck. If you do, draw a card. You can’t use more than 1 Pitch a Pyukumuku Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: It’s covered in a slime that keeps its skin moist, allowing it to stay on land for days without drying up.

Name: Inteleon V
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Inteleon VMAX
Ability-Effect: You must discard a [W] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.
Effects: You may put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Chewtle
Flavor-Text: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

Name: Drednaw
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

Name: Arrokuda
Flavor-Text: After it’s eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That’s when Cramorant swallows it up.

Name: Barraskewda
Flavor-Text: It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.

Name: Snom
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [W] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It eats snow that piles up on the ground. The more snow it eats, the bigger and more impressive the spikes on its back grow.

Name: Frosmoth
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

Name: Pikachu V
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

Name: Electrode
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

Name: Plusle
Effects: If 1 of your Minun used an attack during your last turn, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out its body to create crackling noises.

Name: Minun
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

Name: Shinx
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Luxio
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Rotom
Effects: Discard all Pokémon Tools from all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With a body made of plasma, it can inhabit all sorts of machines. It loves to surprise others.

Name: Tynamo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

Name: Eelektrik
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

Name: Eelektross
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Eelektrik during this turn, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

Name: Helioptile
Flavor-Text: The sun powers this Pokémon’s electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

Name: Charjabug
Flavor-Text: Its digestive processes convert the leaves it eats into electricity. An electric sac in its belly stores the electricity for later use.

Name: Vikavolt
Effects: Discard 2 [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 200 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: If it carries a Charjabug to use as a spare battery, a flying Vikavolt can rapidly fire high-powered beams of electricity.

Name: Zeraora
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It approaches its enemies at the speed of lightning, then tears them limb from limb with its sharp claws.

Name: Boltund V
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Boltund VMAX
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [L] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Max Bolt.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Toxel
Flavor-Text: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.

Name: Toxel
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.

Name: Toxtricity
Effects: You may choose to make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Paralyzed. If you do, this Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This short-tempered and aggressive Pokémon chugs stagnant water to absorb any toxins it might contain.

Name: Toxtricity
Ability-Effect: If all your Pokémon in play are Fusion Strike Pokémon, your opponent’s Pokémon VMAX in play get -30 HP.
Flavor-Text: It has an electrical organ on its chest. While generating electricity, it fills its surroundings with sounds like the strumming of a bass guitar.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It carries electrically roasted seeds with it as if they’re precious treasures. No matter how much it eats, it always gets hungry again in short order.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let’s All Rollout attack.
Flavor-Text: The songs they sing are totally different depending on the region they live in. Some even sound like they’re shouting!

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: When it gets angry, it inhales with all its might, and its body gradually inflates. Sometimes they can grow 20 times larger!

Name: Jynx
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

Name: Mew V
Effects: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mew VMAX
Effects: Choose 1 of your Benched Fusion Strike Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Snubbull
Flavor-Text: In contrast to its appearance, it’s quite timid. When playing with other puppy Pokémon, it sometimes gets bullied.

Name: Granbull
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s popular with young people, Granbull is timid and sensitive, so it’s totally incompetent as a watchdog.

Name: Galarian Corsola
Flavor-Text: Sudden climate change wiped out this ancient kind of Corsola. This Pokémon absorbs others’ life-force through its branches.

Name: Galarian Cursola
Effects: Put a Basic Pokémon V from your opponent’s discard pile onto their Bench. If you do, put damage counters on that Pokémon until its remaining HP is 30.
Flavor-Text: Be cautious of the ectoplasmic body surrounding its soul. You’ll become stiff as stone if you touch it.

Name: Mawile
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] more, and its Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Flavor-Text: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Fusion Strike Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

Name: Munna
Flavor-Text: It eats dreams and releases mist. The mist is pink when it’s eating a good dream, and black when it’s eating a nightmare.

Name: Musharna
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It drowses and dreams all the time. It’s best to leave it be if it’s just woken up, as it’s a terrible grump when freshly roused from sleep.

Name: Sigilyph
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A discovery was made in the desert where Sigilyph fly. The ruins of what may have been an ancient city were found beneath the sands.

Name: Meloetta
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each Fusion Strike Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

Name: Sandygast
Flavor-Text: If you build sand mounds when you’re playing, destroy them before you go home, or they may get possessed and become Sandygast.

Name: Palossand
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Once it has whipped up a sandstorm to halt its opponents in their tracks, this terrifying Pokémon snatches away their vitality.

Name: Indeedee
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: They diligently serve people and Pokémon so they can gather feelings of gratitude. The females are particularly good at babysitting.

Name: Dreepy
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: If this weak Pokémon is by itself, a mere child could defeat it. But if Dreepy has friends to help it train, it can evolve and become much stronger.

Name: Drakloak
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Without a Dreepy to place on its head and care for, it gets so uneasy it’ll try to substitute any Pokémon it finds for the missing Dreepy.

Name: Dragapult
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: Apparently the Dreepy inside Dragapult’s horns eagerly look forward to being launched out at Mach speeds.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: Look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.
This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let’s All Rollout attack.
Flavor-Text: When its skin gets wrinkled from moisture, it heads for a volcano. It lies flat on a spot with a lot of geothermal heat and dries itself out.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: Put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its thick claws, it’s good at climbing trees. There are plenty of Sandslash that park themselves in trees and go right to sleep.

Name: Mankey
Flavor-Text: If one gets angry, all the others around it will get angry, so silence is a rare visitor in a troop of Mankey.

Name: Primeape
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The blood vessels in its brain are sturdier than those of other Pokémon, so it can stay healthy despite its constant raging.

Name: Geodude
Flavor-Text: It uses both hands to climb precipitous cliffs. People who see it in action have been known to take up bouldering.

Name: Graveler
Flavor-Text: It travels by rolling down cliffs. If it falls into a river, it will explode with its last gasp.

Name: Golem
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, put 10 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It detonates its own body. The power from that explosion can propel it up steep mountain paths with amazing speed.

Name: Onix
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

Name: Steelix
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It flies straight at its target’s face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey… Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

Name: Makuhita
Flavor-Text: There’s a rumor of a traditional recipe for stew that Trainers can use to raise strong Makuhita.

Name: Hariyama
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Flavor-Text: Hariyama that are big and fat aren’t necessarily strong. There are some small ones that move nimbly and use moves skillfully.

Name: Baltoy
Flavor-Text: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

Name: Claydol
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Put damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 60.
Flavor-Text: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

Name: Lucario V
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drilbur
Flavor-Text: It’s a digger, using its claws to burrow through the ground. It causes damage to vegetable crops, so many farmers have little love for it.

Name: Landorus
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: From the forces of lightning and wind, it creates energy to give nutrients to the soil and make the land abundant.

Name: Pancham
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [D] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Wanting to make sure it’s taken seriously, Pancham’s always giving others a glare. But if it’s not focusing, it ends up smiling.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.

Name: Bewear
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.

Name: Clobbopus
Flavor-Text: Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn’t alarmed when that happens—it knows they’ll grow back. It’s about as smart as a three-year-old.

Name: Grapploct
Flavor-Text: Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.

Name: Falinks
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 160 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

Name: Falinks
Flavor-Text: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

Name: Gengar V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Put 3 damage counters on this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gengar VMAX
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX in play.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Tyranitar V
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Zigzagoon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon, it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon its surroundings.

Name: Galarian Linoone
Flavor-Text: This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself.

Name: Galarian Obstagoon
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t use that attack.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

Name: Carvanha
Flavor-Text: With its sturdy jaws and fangs, it can easily chomp wooden boats to splinters. It fights with Basculin over food.

Name: Sharpedo
Flavor-Text: It drinks in seawater and jets it from its rear to propel itself. It’s very sensitive to the scent of blood.

Name: Absol
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The elderly call it the disaster Pokémon and detest it, but interest in its power to predict disasters is on the rise.

Name: Croagunk
Flavor-Text: Once diluted, its poison becomes medicinal. This Pokémon came into popularity after a pharmaceutical company chose it as a mascot.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 4 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
Flavor-Text: It booms out a victory croak when its prey goes down in defeat. This Pokémon and Seismitoad are related species.

Name: Darkrai
Flavor-Text: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born from a bag stuffed with trash. Galarian Weezing relish the fumes belched by Trubbish.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.

Name: Zorua
Flavor-Text: If a normally talkative child suddenly stops talking, it may have been replaced by Zorua.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If it thinks humans are going to discover its den, Zoroark shows them visions that make them wander around in the woods.

Name: Vullaby
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Vullaby grow quickly. Bones that have gotten too small for older Vullaby to wear often get passed down to younger ones in the nest.

Name: Mandibuzz
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, Pokémon can’t be played from your opponent’s hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They adorn themselves with bones. There seem to be fashion trends among them, as different bones come into and fall out of popularity.

Name: Pangoro
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.

Name: Yveltal
Flavor-Text: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

Name: Impidimp
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It sneaks into people’s homes, stealing things and feasting on the negative energy of the frustrated occupants.

Name: Morgrem
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With sly cunning, it tries to lure people into the woods. Some believe it to have the power to make crops grow.

Name: Grimmsnarl
Effects: If you have 2 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its hairs work like muscle fibers. When its hairs unfurl, they latch on to opponents, ensnaring them as tentacles would.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Hunger hormones affect its temperament. Until its hunger is appeased, it gets up to all manner of evil deeds.

Name: Galarian Meowth
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: These daring Pokémon have coins on their foreheads. Darker coins are harder, and harder coins garner more respect among Meowth.

Name: Galarian Perrserker
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: After many battles, it evolved dangerous claws that come together to form daggers when extended.

Name: Skarmory
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its body is draped in steel armor. It looks heavy, but it can fly at speeds of up to 185 miles an hour!

Name: Excadrill
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Known as the Drill King, this Pokémon can tunnel through the terrain at speeds of over 90 mph.

Name: Durant
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [R] Pokémon, it is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

Name: Genesect V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have as many cards in your hand as you have Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Klefki
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: Long ago it lived in mines, but once the minerals that make up its diet became scarcer, Klefki began appearing in human settlements.

Name: Togedemaru
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let’s All Rollout attack.
Flavor-Text: When it’s in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

Name: Meltan
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.

Name: Melmetal
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon that have an Ability.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Revered long ago for its capacity to create iron from nothing, for some reason it has come back to life after 3,000 years.

Name: Corviknight
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With their great intellect and flying skills, these Pokémon very successfully act as the Galar region’s airborne taxi service.

Name: Cufant
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: If a job requires serious strength, this Pokémon will excel at it. Its copper body tarnishes in the rain, turning a vibrant green color.

Name: Copperajah
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon live in herds. Their trunks have incredible grip strength, strong enough to crush giant rocks into powder.

Name: Latias
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [P] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Latios.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.
Flavor-Text: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.

Name: Latios
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [P] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Latias.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

Name: Goomy
Flavor-Text: Their horns are powerful sensors. As soon as Goomy pick up any sign of enemies, they go into hiding. This is how they’ve survived.

Name: Sliggoo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The lump on its back contains its tiny brain. It thinks only of food and escaping its enemies.

Name: Goodra
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent tries to attach an Energy card from their hand to a Pokémon, they must flip a coin. If tails, your opponent discards that Energy card instead of attaching it, and this doesn’t use up their Energy attachment for the turn.
Flavor-Text: Its form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer.

Name: Turtonator
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Eating sulfur in its volcanic habitat is what causes explosive compounds to develop in its shell. Its droppings are also dangerously explosive.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It washes its face regularly to keep the coin on its forehead spotless. It doesn’t get along with Galarian Meowth.

Name: Persian
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Its elegant and refined behavior clashes with that of the barbaric Perrserker. The relationship between the two is one of mutual disdain.

Name: Dodrio V
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s attacks do 80 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because the eggs on their bellies have been overharvested by people in the past, the Chansey population remains very small.

Name: Blissey
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to each of your Pokémon that has the Let’s All Rollout attack by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon VMAX.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let’s All Rollout attack.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is overflowing with love. Only Chansey that share a strong bond with their Trainer can evolve, so people say.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: You shouldn’t get close to the child when it’s playing outside its mother’s pouch. Its mother is always nearby watching over it.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

Name: Snorlax
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

Name: Dunsparce
Ability-Effect: [C] Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: When it sees a person, it digs a hole with its tail to make its escape. If you happen to find one, consider yourself lucky.

Name: Stantler
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.

Name: Smeargle
Effects: Put up to 2 Fusion Strike Trainer cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.

Name: Skitty
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

Name: Delcatty
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

Name: Buneary
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The reason it keeps one ear rolled up is so it can launch a swift counterattack if it’s attacked by an enemy.

Name: Lopunny
Flavor-Text: It’s notably wary and has a dislike of fighting, but at the same time, it can deliver powerful kicks with its lithe legs.

Name: Bunnelby
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It’s very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight’s flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It always strikes a pose before going for its finishing move. Sometimes opponents take advantage of that time to counterattack.

Name: Greedent V
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Draw 3 cards.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Greedent VMAX
Effects: If your opponent’s Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards.
Draw 3 cards.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Rookidee
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Jumping nimbly about, this small-bodied Pokémon takes advantage of even the slightest opportunity to disorient larger opponents.

Name: Corvisquire
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The lessons of many harsh battles have taught it how to accurately judge an opponent’s strength.

Name: Wooloo
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Let’s All Rollout attack.
Flavor-Text: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won’t be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

Name: Wooloo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won’t be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

Name: Dubwool
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

Name: Adventurer’s Discovery
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Battle VIP Pass
Description: You can use this card only during your first turn.Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Bug Catcher
Description: Draw 2 cards. Flip a coin. If heads, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Chili & Cilan & Cress
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Fusion Strike Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Cook
Description: Heal 70 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Cram-o-matic
Description: You can use this card only if you discard another Item card from your hand.Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Cross Switcher
Description: You must play 2 Cross Switcher cards at once. (This effect works one time for 2 cards.)Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Crossceiver
Description: You must play 2 Crossceiver cards at once. (This effect works one time for 2 cards.)Put a Pokémon or a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Dancer
Description: Draw 2 cards. If you go second and it’s your first turn, draw 3 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Elesa’s Sparkle
Description: Choose up to 2 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fusion Strike Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Farewell Bell
Description: If the Pokémon VMAX this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Judge
Description: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Power Tablet
Description: During this turn, your Fusion Strike Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Quick Ball
Description: You can play this card only if you discard another card from your hand.Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Schoolboy
Description: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 1, 3, or 5 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Schoolgirl
Description: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Shauna
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Sidney
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard up to 2 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards, Special Energy cards, and Stadium cards from it.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Skaters’ Park
Description: Whenever either player’s Active Pokémon retreats, put any basic Energy that would be discarded into their hand instead of the discard pile.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Spongy Gloves
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active [W] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Fusion Strike Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to a Fusion Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Fusion Strike Pokémon, discard this card.As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to the Pokémon this card is attached to.

Name: Celebi V
Effects: Attach any number of [G] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tsareena V
Effects: You may discard any number of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chandelure V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 40 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Crabominable V
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Boltund V
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mew V
Effects: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mew V
Effects: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sandaconda V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hoopa V
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [P] and [D] type.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on 1 of your Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Genesect V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have as many cards in your hand as you have Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Genesect V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have as many cards in your hand as you have Fusion Strike Pokémon in play.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Greedent V
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Draw 3 cards.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Greedent V
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Draw 3 cards.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chili & Cilan & Cress
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Fusion Strike Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Dancer
Description: Draw 2 cards. If you go second and it’s your first turn, draw 3 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Elesa’s Sparkle
Description: Choose up to 2 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fusion Strike Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Schoolboy
Description: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 1, 3, or 5 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Schoolgirl
Description: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Shauna
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Sidney
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard up to 2 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards, Special Energy cards, and Stadium cards from it.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Chandelure VMAX
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t play any Pokémon Tool cards from their hand.
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 70 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Inteleon VMAX
Ability-Effect: You must discard a [W] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and put 2 damage counters on each of them.
Effects: You may put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Boltund VMAX
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [L] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Max Bolt.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mew VMAX
Effects: Choose 1 of your Benched Fusion Strike Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mew VMAX
Effects: Choose 1 of your Benched Fusion Strike Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Espeon VMAX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon that have Energy attached. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Gengar VMAX
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX in play.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Greedent VMAX
Effects: If your opponent’s Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards.
Draw 3 cards.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Chili & Cilan & Cress
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Fusion Strike Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Dancer
Description: Draw 2 cards. If you go second and it’s your first turn, draw 3 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Elesa’s Sparkle
Description: Choose up to 2 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Fusion Strike Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Schoolboy
Description: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 1, 3, or 5 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Schoolgirl
Description: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Shauna
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Sidney
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard up to 2 in any combination of Pokémon Tool cards, Special Energy cards, and Stadium cards from it.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Flaaffy
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [L] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

Name: Power Tablet
Description: During this turn, your Fusion Strike Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Training Court
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may put a basic Energy card from their discard pile into their hand.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.
